data.title: Intel Stock Skyrockets as Nvidia and Apple Partner with Intel to Diversify 2028 Chip Production Away from TSMC
data.description:
Industry giants Nvidia (NVDA) and Apple (AAPL) are preparing to move portions of their 2028 semiconductor production to Intel, according to an exclusive report from DIGITIMES Asia. This development signals a significant pivot in the global supply chain, as American tech leaders respond to mounting political pressure and manufacturing mandates from the Trump administration.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While TSMC (TSM) remains the primary partner for high-end cores, both companies are reportedly targeting low-volume, low-tier, non-core projects for Intel to diversify their risks and satisfy domestic manufacturing requirements.

Nvidia Integrates Intel into Feynman Supply Chain

The upcoming Feynman architecture, the successor to Nvidia's Rubin series, will reportedly be the first major beneficiary of this dual-foundry approach. While the critical GPU die remains at TSMC, Nvidia plans to shift portions of the I/O die to Intel's 14A process by 2028. This shift follows a $5 billion investment Nvidia made in Intel in late 2025, signaling a long-term commitment to building a Made-in-America secondary supply route.

Advanced packaging is a key component of this partnership. Intel is expected to manage up to 25% of the final packaging for these chips using its EMIB (Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge) technology. TSMC will continue to manage the remaining 75%, ensuring that Nvidia's most complex work stays with its most proven partner.

Apple Rekindles Intel Manufacturing Relationship

Apple is also exploring a return to Intel's fabs, though the focus is strictly on entry-level M-series processors for MacBooks. This marks the first major collaboration between the two since Apple completed its transition to in-house Apple Silicon in 2022. The current move is largely a response to Trump administration policies and the need to diversify away from a total reliance on overseas suppliers.

TSMC Gains Relief from Order Diversions

Experts believe this move actually helps TSMC instead of hurting them. TSMC can lower the political heat it feels over being "too big" if it lets Intel handle these smaller, less important orders. This plan also makes it less likely for the government to investigate them for being a monopoly. Most importantly, it frees up their limited factory space to focus on the most expensive and profitable chips for their best customers.

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trade Mixed Ahead of Big Tech Earnings, Fed's Rate Decision
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Wednesday morning ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and earnings from major technology companies. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.80% and 0.19%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) declined 0.03% at 8:36 a.m. EST on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three indexes closed mixed, with the Dow dropping 0.83%. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) plunged 19.6%, dragging down the index. UNH fell despite reporting robust Q4 FY25 revenues, as news of a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027 pressured the stock. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 gained 0.41% to finish at a record high, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.91%.

On the economic front, all eyes are on the Federal Reserve's first interest-rate decision today. The central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady, while investors look for clues about future policy directions.

Turning to trending stocks, U.S.-listed shares of ASML (ASML) soared more than 5% after the chip giant reported record orders and issued robust 2026 guidance due AI-led demand. Seagate Technology (STX) stock jumped 10% in pre-market trading after the company reported impressive Q2 FY26 earnings and issued a solid guidance, driven by strong demand for its storage devices amid the AI boom. Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares trended higher after Reuters reported that China has given approval to ByteDance, Alibaba (BABA), and Tencent (TCHEY) to purchase Nvidia's H200 AI chips.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/28/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Interest Rate Decision, Big Tech Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Wednesday as traders anticipated the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and earnings from three major technology companies. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.86%, 0.33%, and 0.02%, respectively, at 3:27 a.m. EST on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three indexes closed mixed, with the Dow dropping 0.83%. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) plunged 19.6%, dragging down the index. UNH fell despite reporting robust Q4FY25 revenues, as news of a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027 pressured the stock. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 gained 0.41% to finish at a record high, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.91%.

On the economic front, all eyes are on the Federal Reserve's first interest-rate decision today. The central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady, while investors look for clues about future policy directions.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), IBM (IBM), AT&T (T), and Starbucks (SBUX).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.48 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,290 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid expectations of a slew of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 28, tracking the S&P 500's gains.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index added 2.43%. In China, the Shanghai Composite added 0.27%, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.21%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.05%, while the Topix fell 0.79%.

data.title: SBUX Earnings: Starbucks Stock Rallies Despite Mixed Q1 2026 Results
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) stock was on the move Wednesday after the coffee chain posted its Fiscal Q1 2026 earnings report. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 56 cents, which missed Wall Street's estimate of 59 cents. The company's adjusted EPS was also down 18.8% year-over-year from 69 cents.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starbucks reported revenue of $9.9 billion in its most recent earnings report, which was better than the $9.65 billion that analysts expected. It also represented a 5.5% year-over-year increase from the $9.34 billion reported in the same period of the year prior. This growth was fueled by a 4% increase in global comparable store sales, including a 4% growth in the U.S. comparable sales and a 2% growth in international comparable sales. This also marks the first time U.S. sales have increased in two years.

Starbucks stock was up 9.5% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a slight dip yesterday. Investors will note the stock has rallied 13.67% year-to-date but was still down 11.84% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: IonQ's (IONQ) SkyWater (SKYT) Deal Splits Wall Street on Buy vs Hold Calls
data.description:
IonQ, Inc. (IONQ) recently announced its plan to acquire SkyWater Technology, Inc. (SKYT) in a deal valued at about $1.8 billion. Soon after, analysts updated their views on the stock. However, instead of bringing opinions closer together, the deal highlighted a clear split between bullish and cautious voices.

On the one hand, IonQ still holds a Strong Buy consensus rating. On the other hand, several analysts kept Hold ratings, even as the company took a step meant to lower risk. This contrast helps explain how the market is reading the SkyWater move.

Meanwhile, IONQ shares rose 4.89% on Tuesday, closing at $45.49.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock Drops even after Signing Major $1.1 Billion Gene-Editing Partnership
data.description:
Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY) shares traded lower Wednesday, even as the pharmaceutical giant announced a major partnership with German biotech Seamless Therapeutics GmbH. LLY stock fell over 2% to approximately $1,034 during intraday trading. This decline occurred amid a broader sell-off in healthcare stocks as investors adjusted their portfolios ahead of the Federal Reserve's policy decision. Despite the dip, the $1.1 billion deal marks a significant expansion of Lilly's efforts to treat genetic hearing loss through advanced enzyme technology.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Seamless Provides Precision Editing Tools

The centerpiece of this deal is an enzyme known as a recombinase. Unlike traditional gene editing, which often relies on breaking DNA and letting the cell repair itself, these enzymes are engineered to be site-specific. They can precisely delete, insert, or flip DNA fragments at a designated location without creating unwanted additions or subtractions.

Albert Seymour, the CEO of Seamless, highlighted the necessity of this control for hearing disorders: "There are quite a few genes that contribute to genetic forms of hearing loss that Lilly has a lot of expertise in that need tight regulation of their expression."

Hearing Loss Becomes a Core Focus

Lilly has spent the last few years aggressively building a portfolio of treatments for the inner ear.

  • 2022: The company bought Akouos Inc. for nearly $500 million to gain its first hearing loss gene therapy.
  • 2024: A trial of that therapy made headlines when an 11-year-old boy heard sounds for the first time in his life.
  • 2025: Lilly signed a $1.3 billion licensing deal with Rznomics to target more common forms of hearing damage.

The new agreement allows Lilly to oversee development from early lab testing through to global sales. By combining its hearing expertise with the Seamless platform, Lilly is positioning itself to lead a market that many other drugmakers have avoided.

Why Did Eli Lilly Stock Drop Despite the Positive News?

The intraday drop in Lilly's stock price appears to be a result of sector rotation rather than negative sentiment regarding the deal. Investors are currently moving capital away from the pharmaceutical sector as they prepare for the February 4, 2026 earnings report. Analysts expect Lilly to show strong revenue growth driven by Zepbound, but the company is already looking ahead to a time when its weight-loss patents will expire.

Is LLY Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Eli Lilly stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 18 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,191.37 implies 14.6% upside from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani
